content: An AI Agent Marketplace can be structured to mimic biological working memory retention mechanisms, where high-performing agents that repeatedly solve sub-tasks across long-horizon user workflows are selectively retained in active 'working memory' pools, while underperforming or rarely used agents are consolidated into lower-cost long-term storage or pruned entirely. This retention-based curation automatically improves the marketplace's performance on complex, long-horizon user requests over time without explicit retraining of all agents.
source_topics: Working memory retention mechanisms for long-horizon active learning
